forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the Bight will move slowly eastwards over the weekend to be centred over the Tasman Sea on Monday. A trough is forecast to enter the far west on Tuesday before sliding over southern parts of SA on Wednesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Possible light showers about windward coasts and ranges and the Lower South East. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures below average in the south and west, grading to well below average in the northeast. South to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the west. Local afternoon coastal sea breezes.
Dry apart from a slight chance of light showers about windward coasts and ranges. South to southeasterly winds, tending easterly nearer the WA border.
Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures remaining below average across most of the state, but nearer average in the far west. Generally southeast to northeasterly winds.

Dry apart from a chance of showers developing in the far west. Maximum temperatures near to slightly above average about the west and the southern agricultural area, grading to below average in the northeast. Generally east to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Isolated showers and possible thunderstorms extending across the west, possibly reaching central districts at night. Maximum temperatures near to slightly above average. Northerly winds ahead of a southwesterly change in the west later.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Less than 2mm, with highly variable totals of 5 to 30 mm from showers and storms associated with the trough moving into the west on Tuesday.
